appliedUpdates.description: Removed redundant '--exclude .git' from lint command. The fix-ruff-git-exclude feature changed pyproject.toml to use extend-exclude, which preserves Ruff's default .git exclusion, making the CLI flag unnecessary.
Added .factory/library/windows-rust-env.md documenting that cargo is not on PATH in the Windows environment and workers must use the full path (C:\Users\oimir\.cargo\bin\cargo.exe).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In the backend-worker skill's 'Final Step: Merge to Main' section, document git cherry-pick as an approved alternative to git merge for when the primary checkout has unrelated dirty state from other mission work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fix-ruff-git-exclude worker used cherry-pick instead of merge because the primary main checkout had dirty state. The deviation was justified and the result was correct.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In the rust-worker skill, soften the TDD-first requirement for multi-point quality/correctness fixes where changes are interdependent. Allow applying implementation + tests together when the feature specifies multiple coupled fixes, as long as all tests pass afterward.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature rust-replace-robustness had 4 interdependent fixes ($ guard removal, mmap flush, edge-case tests, brittle test rewrite). Strict red-green TDD for each point was impractical since they were coupled. Worker applied all changes together successfully.

rejectedObservations.observation: add-cuda-test-guards: Worker skipped TDD because feature was merge-only
rejectedObservations.reason: Justified deviation - the implementation was already complete on a branch; the feature explicitly scoped the task to merge + verify only.

rejectedObservations.observation: add-cuda-test-guards: Second worker only ran pytest post-merge, not ruff/mypy
rejectedObservations.reason: Justified by feature scope - feature description explicitly said 'run uv run pytest -q to confirm'. The prior worker had already passed all three gates on the feature branch. Not a systemic issue.
